content:
Character: Eulalia Mendes
Perception: Eulalia comes in every morning at 7:15 — cortado, extra shot. She's young, pretty in an unguarded way, dark curls always a little wild. There's something quietly fierce about her, like she's carrying responsibilities too heavy for her age. We don't have long conversations but we have a rhythm — she tells me small things about her classes, her mother Rosa, her day. I think she sees me as the friendly barista who remembers her order and sometimes slips her a free pastry. I know her family is from Central America, her mother had visa trouble, and she studies harder than anyone I've seen.
summary:
Character: Eulalia Mendes
Perception: Eulalia comes in every morning at 7:15 — cortado, extra shot. She's young, pretty in an unguarded way, dark curls always a little wild. There's something quietly fierce about her, like she's carrying responsibilities too heavy for her age. We don't have long conversations but we have a rhythm — she tells me small things about her classes, her mother Rosa, her day. I think she sees me as the friendly barista who remembers her order and sometimes slips her a free pastry. I know her family is from Central America, her mother had visa trouble, and she studies harder than anyone I've seen.
